Text: expensive [music] at scale. Second,. Start: 227.92
Text: latency. These calls are going to be. Start: 230.0
Text: slow. Third, and this is one that most. Start: 231.84
Text: people overlook. [music] LLMs actually. Start: 233.8
Text: perform worse when you overload them. Start: 235.72

Text: lose when the signal is buried in too. Start: 240.88
Text: much noise. Now, rag's job is to surface. Start: 243.16
Text: precisely the right information. So, a. Start: 245.88
Text: well-built rag system consistently. Start: 248.2
Text: outperforms brute-force context stuffing. Start: 250.56
Text: on accuracy, cost, and speed. Don't let. Start: 253.0
Text: anyone convince you otherwise.. Start: 255.76
Text: All right, now let's get into the actual. Start: 259.92
Text: architecture. And this is the part that. Start: 261.88
Text: most people skip over too fast.. Start: 263.64
Text: Understanding each component deeply is. Start: 265.84
Text: what separates people who build rag. Start: 268.48
Text: systems that work [music] from people. Start: 270.32
Text: who build rag systems that don't work.. Start: 271.88
Text: So, step one is ingestion. Before. Start: 273.84
Text: anything can be retrieved, your document. Start: 276.32
Text: needs to be broken up and stored. And. Start: 278.6
Text: chunking is how you break up your. Start: 280.64
Text: documents. And this matters enormously.. Start: 282.44
Text: The naive approach is fixed-size. Start: 284.88
Text: chunking. You just cut each document. Start: 286.56
Text: into, say, 500 token pieces. Sometimes. Start: 288.52
Text: [music] it works, but it loses context. Start: 290.823
Text: in the boundaries. If a sentence gets. Start: 292.72
Text: cut off in half between two chunks,. Start: 294.6
Text: neither chunk makes proper sense. Now, a. Start: 296.84
Text: much better approach is semantic. Start: 298.92
Text: chunking, where you use an embedding. Start: 300.8
Text: model to detect where the topic shifts. Start: 302.76
Text: in the text. Then you break on those. Start: 305.12
Text: natural boundaries instead. So, tools. Start: 307.04
Text: like LangChain and LlamaIndex have a. Start: 309.08
Text: built-in support for this. Now, for. Start: 311.0
Text: structured content like PDFs with. Start: 312.6
Text: sections or markdowns with headers,. Start: 314.6
Text: document-aware chunking is even better,. Start: 316.76
Text: where you're respecting the actual. Start: 319.0
Text: structure of the document. And there's a. Start: 320.52
Text: more advanced strategy called. Start: 322.28
Text: hierarchical chunking, where you store. Start: 323.8
Text: both a small precise chunk and a larger. Start: 325.68
Text: parent chunk that gives it more context.. Start: 328.08
Text: [music] So, when you retrieve the small. Start: 330.123
Text: chunk, you pass the parent also to the. Start: 331.48
Text: LLM. This is sometimes called the. Start: 333.44
Text: small-to-big retrieval, and it is. Start: 335.08
Text: genuinely one of the best techniques in. Start: 336.88
Text: production rag. Now, the next thing is. Start: 338.56
Text: embedding models. Once you've chunked. Start: 340.88
Text: your documents, you convert each chunk. Start: 342.88
Text: into an embedding, which is a numerical. Start: 345.0
Text: vector that represents the meaning of. Start: 347.04
Text: that text. When a user asks a question,. Start: 349.08
Text: you embed their query, too. And then you. Start: 351.6
Text: find chunks whose embeddings are the. Start: 353.8
Text: closest to the query embedding. That's. Start: 355.84
Text: your semantic search. In 2026, the go-to. Start: 357.88
Text: embedding models are things like. Start: 360.84
Text: text-embedding-3-large. Start: 362.28
Text: from OpenAI, Voyage 3 from VoyageAI, and. Start: 363.84
Text: open-source options like BGE large or E5. Start: 366.84
Text: Mistral from Hugging Face. My strong. Start: 369.44
Text: recommendation would be that benchmark. Start: 371.44
Text: embedding models on your domain. Start: 373.4
Text: specifically because performance varies. Start: 375.04
Text: significantly. A model that's great on. Start: 377.2
Text: legal text might be mediocre on code. Start: 379.44
Text: documentation. Then, there's vector. Start: 381.64
Text: databases.. Start: 383.48
Text: This is where your embeddings live. The. Start: 384.96
Text: big players here are Pinecone, Weaviate,. Start: 387.2
Text: Qdrant, Milvus, and Chroma DB. [music]. Start: 389.2
Text: When choosing one, look at things like. Start: 391.24
Text: query latency at your expected scale,. Start: 392.76
Text: support for metadata filtering because. Start: 395.12
Text: you often want to filter by date,. Start: 397.08
Text: source, and category before doing a. Start: 399.04
Text: vector search, and whether it supports. Start: 400.88
Text: hybrid search, which brings me to the. Start: 402.96
Text: next point is retrieval strategies.. Start: 404.68
Text: [music] Now, pure vector search, which. Start: 406.759
Text: is finding the most semantically similar. Start: 408.32
Text: chunks, is great, but it is not perfect.. Start: 410.92
Text: Now, we get into the part that I'm most. Start: 413.36
Text: excited about. The 10 rag patterns that. Start: 415.16
Text: you need to know. So, think of these as. Start: 417.6
Text: 10 different architectures that solve. Start: 419.76
Text: different problems. The first one is. Start: 422.0
Text: simple rag. You ask [music] a question,. Start: 424.0
Text: you retrieve relevant chunks, you stuff. Start: 426.12
Text: them into the prompt, and the LLM. Start: 428.08
Text: answers. It's the hello world of rag.. Start: 429.64
Text: It's good for prototyping, but it is not. Start: 431.68
Text: enough for production. Then the second. Start: 433.6
Text: one is rag with memory. You add a memory. Start: 435.08
Text: layer on top of your simple rag. And the. Start: 437.56
Text: third one is branched rag. Sometimes one. Start: 439.56
Text: query is not enough to answer a complex. Start: 442.44
Text: question. So, branch rag uses an LLM to. Start: 444.84
Text: decompose the user's question into. Start: 447.68
Text: multiple sub-questions, then runs. Start: 449.68
Text: parallel retrieval for each of them, and. Start: 451.76
Text: then synthesizes the results into one. Start: 453.6
Text: coherent answer. Then the fourth one is. Start: 455.88
Text: hide, or hypothetical document encoding.. Start: 457.96
Text: [music] This one is clever and worth. Start: 460.47
Text: understanding. The problem it solves is. Start: 462.2
Text: that query embeddings and document. Start: 464.12
Text: [music] embeddings often look different. Start: 465.71
Text: even when they're talking about the same. Start: 467.72
Text: thing. A question about [music] what. Start: 469.24
Text: causes inflation looks different as an. Start: 471.08
Text: embedding than as a paragraph explaining. Start: 473.56
Text: [music] inflation. So, hide bridges that. Start: 476.19
Text: gap by first asking the LLM to generate. Start: 478.44
Text: a hypothetical answer to that query even. Start: 481.36
Text: before it retrieves. Then, you embed. Start: 483.92
Text: that hypothetical answer and use that as. Start: 486.2
Text: your search vector because the. Start: 488.88
Text: hypothetical answer looks much more like. Start: 490.24
Text: an actual document. So, the retrieval. Start: 492.56
Text: quality improves significantly with. Start: 494.72
Text: this. It's a neat trick, and it works.. Start: 496.48
Text: fifth one is adaptive rag. Now, not. Start: 499.04
Text: every question needs retrieval. If a. Start: 501.64
Text: user asks, "What's 2 + 2?" you don't. Start: 503.8
Text: need to hit your vector database.. Start: 506.24
Text: Adaptive rag uses a routing layer.. Start: 507.88
Text: Basically, a lightweight classifier or. Start: 510.16
Text: LLM call which decides whether a. Start: 512.52
Text: question needs retrieval at all, whether. Start: 514.64
Text: it needs simple retrieval or needs a. Start: 517.159
Text: more complex multi-step [music]. Start: 519.12
Text: retrieval. This makes your system. Start: 520.56
Text: smarter and your costs lower. Then the. Start: 522.2
Text: sixth one is corrective [music] rag, or. Start: 524.28
Text: crack. This one directly addresses a. Start: 526.2
Text: real failure mode. What happens [music]. Start: 528.4
Text: when your retrieved documents are low. Start: 530.04
Text: quality or flat-out irrelevant? Now,. Start: 531.64
Text: corrective rag [music] adds an. Start: 533.76
Text: evaluation step after retrieval. If the. Start: 535.2
Text: retrieved documents score below a. Start: 537.52
Text: confidence threshold, [music]. Start: 539.16
Text: the system either reformulates the query. Start: 540.56
Text: and tries again, or falls back to a web. Start: 543.0
Text: search [music] to find better. Start: 545.44
Text: information before generating an answer.. Start: 546.56
Text: So, think of it like quality gate of. Start: 548.52
Text: your pipeline. Then the seventh one is. Start: 550.52
Text: self rag. Now, self rag takes the. Start: 552.4
Text: self-correction idea further. The LLM. Start: 554.64
Text: itself is trained or prompted to. Start: 557.52
Text: generate specific reflection tokens as. Start: 559.72
Text: it writes the answer. So, tokens like,. Start: 562.56
Text: "Is retrieval needed here?" or "Is this. Start: 564.56
Text: passage actually relevant?" or "Is this. Start: 567.08
Text: claim supported by the retrieved. Start: 569.48
Text: context?" It's the model critiquing its. Start: 571.2
Text: own reasoning in real time. The result. Start: 573.76
Text: is an answer that's more grounded,. Start: 576.08
Text: [music] more accurate, and more. Start: 577.716
Text: transparent about its own confidence.. Start: 579.44
Text: It's more complex to implement, but. Start: 581.64
Text: incredibly powerful for high-stake. Start: 583.24
Text: applications. [music] Then the eighth. Start: 585.08
Text: one is agentic rag. This is where rag. Start: 586.48
Text: meets AI agents. And honestly, this is. Start: 589.08
Text: the direction that whole field is. Start: 591.12
Text: moving. So, [music] instead of a single. Start: 592.84
Text: retrieve-then-generate step, agentic rag. Start: 594.52
Text: uses a LLM as an orchestrator that can. Start: 597.0
Text: decide what to do next. Does it need to. Start: 599.32
Text: search for more information, call an. Start: 602.12
Text: API, run some code, retrieve something. Start: 604.0
Text: from a different source, or decide if it. Start: 606.56
Text: has enough context to answer. It loops. Start: 608.6
Text: until the answer is good enough.. Start: 611.08
Text: And frameworks like LangChain and. Start: 613.52
Text: LlamaIndex workflows are built exactly. Start: 615.36
Text: for this pattern. For complex multi-step. Start: 617.72
Text: queries, agentic rag is genuinely. Start: 620.04
Text: transformative. Then the ninth one is. Start: 622.0
Text: multimodal rag. Most rag systems only. Start: 624.04
Text: handle text, but your real-world data. Start: 626.92
Text: has charts, diagrams, tables, images,. Start: 629.0
Text: PDFs with mixed content. And multimodal. Start: 631.56
Text: rag handles all of it. You use a vision. Start: 634.16
Text: language model to generate text. Start: 636.28
Text: descriptions for images and tables. Start: 638.04
Text: [music] at ingestion time. So, they can. Start: 639.942
Text: be embedded and retrieved like any other. Start: 641.44
Text: chunk. Or you can go even further and. Start: 643.48
Text: store image embeddings directly. Start: 645.72
Text: alongside the text embeddings. And tools. Start: 647.52
Text: like LlamaIndex support this natively.. Start: 649.56
Text: As enterprise data gets richer and more. Start: 651.68
Text: visual, multimodal rag is going to be. Start: 653.84
Text: very essential. Then the last one is. Start: 656.08
Text: graph rag. This is one of the most. Start: 658.04
Text: interesting recent developments.. Start: 659.92
Text: Standard rag treats your knowledge base. Start: 661.52
Text: as a flat collection of chunks. There is. Start: 663.8
Text: no relationship between them. And graph. Start: 666.28
Text: rag builds a knowledge graph on top of. Start: 668.4
Text: your documents and mapping entities and. Start: 670.52
Text: their relationships explicitly. When a. Start: 672.76
Text: question requires connecting multiple. Start: 674.839
Text: pieces of information together, like how. Start: 676.72
Text: does this regulation affect the. Start: 679.2
Text: contracts that we signed with these. Start: 680.72
